abstract: The Microbead-Free Waters Act of 2015 (Public Law 114-114) amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to ban rinse-off cosmetics containing intentionally-added plastic microbeads, with the ban effective beginning January 1, 2018, and manufacturing restrictions to follow.
ban rinse-off cosmetics that contain intentionally-added plastic microbeads beginning on January 1, 2018
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
